mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing S&P 500 valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate reflects deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening aggregate demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in > 60% of months where UNRATE increases, or point-biserial correlation with return direction is < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales exceeding a 0.5% monthly growth threshold signals robust consumer spending momentum, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances yield negative 20-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during periods of monetary tightening.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declining below its 12-month moving average indicates a broad supply-side contraction and manufacturing weakness, which suppresses corporate profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered months, or the threshold condition fails to separate return distributions (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of federal funds rates above 5.0% and a rising unemployment rate creates stagflationary pressure that simultaneously raises financing costs and weakens earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression, or combined triggers produce positive returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated consumer price indices and robust retail sales demonstrate strong consumer pricing power and demand resilience, which protects corporate margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict positive returns (accuracy < 55%), or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth exceeding 150,000 per month during a confirmed economic expansion signals sustained labor market strength, which boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during late-cycle phases, or forward 60-day returns are negative in > 60% of triggered instances when GDPC1 growth decelerates.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in housing starts indicate a sharp pullback in residential investment demand, which reduces construction activity and consumer wealth effects, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Consecutive housing declines are followed by positive 20-day equity returns in > 55% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS is below 3.0%.
